mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk, triggering risk-off positioning that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, positive 5-day forward returns occur more than 50% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive federal funds rates above 2% and elevated consumer price index levels above 210 jointly elevates real discount rates and compresses profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly triggered quarters produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Deceleration in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals impending supply-driven earnings compression as capacity utilization peaks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns do not occur in more than 50% of late-cycle INDPRO deceleration episodes during a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential investment and downstream consumer durable demand, which reduces cyclical earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered months exhibit positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment and industrial production reflect a broad-based economic acceleration that exceeds the signal from either series in isolation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates maintained during a monetary tightening cycle increase the probability of an overtightening policy error, prompting negative short-term equity repricing over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return is non-negative during tightening cycle periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falling below 110 signals acute supply-side disruption and an imminent negative earnings shock, causing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered weeks exhibit positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.
